title: Qila Mubarak, Patiala
tagline: Qila Mubarak, Patiala — heritage city of Punjab

overview.short: Qila Mubarak, is a fortress of Sikh architecture in Patiala, Punjab, India.
overview.description: Qila Mubarak, is a fortress of Sikh architecture in Patiala, Punjab, India. It was the residential palace of the Maharaja of Patiala. It is mostly disused now and in poor condition. Much of the fortress is closed-off to public access, including its Shish Mahal.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: North Zone Cultural Centre
topPlaces.description: North Zone Cultural Centre or NZCC (ISO: Uttara Kṣētra Sāṁskr̥tika Kēṁdra) in Patiala in Punjab state was first among several regional cultural centres established by Government of India to preserve and promote arts, crafts, traditions and cultural heritage of the Indian states of Punjab, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Haryana, Rajasthan, as well as the union territories of Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh and Chandigarh. Establishment of North Zone Culture Centre was announced by then Prime Minister of India, Rajiv Gandhi during his visit to Hussainiwala, Punjab on 23 March 1985.

topPlaces.name: Shri Kali Devi Temple, Patiala
topPlaces.description: Shri Kali Devi Temple is a Hindu temple in Patiala, Punjab, India. It was built by the Maharaja of Patiala in 1936.

topPlaces.name: Patiala
topPlaces.description: Patiala (Punjabi pronunciation: [pʌʈeɑ̈ːɭɑ]) is a city in southeastern Punjab, northwestern India. It is the fourth largest city in the state and is the administrative capital of Patiala district.

topPlaces.name: Gurdwara Dukh Nivaran Sahib
topPlaces.description: Gurdwara Dukh Nivaran Sahib is situated in what used to be the village of Lehal, now part of Patiala city.

topPlaces.name: Patiala Branch
topPlaces.description: The Patiala Branch, also known as the Patiala Feeder, is a canal branch of the Sirhind Canal located in Punjab, India. It is one of the two main bifurcations of the Sirhind Canal, with the other being the Combined Branch.

topPlaces.name: Sanaur
topPlaces.description: Sanaur is a town and a municipal council in Patiala in the Indian state of Punjab.

topPlaces.name: Career Academy, Patiala
topPlaces.description: Career Academy School, Patiala is a higher-secondary co-education private school in the Patiala city of Punjab, India. The school was founded in 2000 and is affiliated to the Central Board of Secondary Education of India.

topPlaces.name: Sidhuwal
topPlaces.description: Sidhuwal is a village on the road Patiala Bhadson approximately six kilometres from Patiala in Patiala tehsil in the Patiala district of the state of Punjab in northwest India. It is located 6 km towards North from District headquarters Patiala and 66 km from State capital Chandigarh.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Qila Mubarak, Patiala?
faq.a: The best time to visit Qila Mubarak, Patiala is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Qila Mubarak, Patiala?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chandigarh International Airport (54 km) and the nearest railway station is Ambala Cantt Junction (42 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Qila Mubarak, Patiala?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Qila Mubarak, Patiala at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Qila Mubarak, Patiala?
faq.a: Stays in Qila Mubarak, Patiala start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Qila Mubarak, Patiala?
faq.a: The top attractions are North Zone Cultural Centre, Shri Kali Devi Temple, Patiala, Patiala, Gurdwara Dukh Nivaran Sahib, Patiala Branch.
